components.role: High-gain inverting amplifier — drives signal well past diode threshold for hard clipping
Positive hard clipping diode — clamps output to ground + Vf
Negative hard clipping diode — clamps output to ground - Vf
Feedback resistor — sets open-loop gain before diode clamping
Input resistor — sets gain and input impedance
Output resistor — current limits the diodes and provides isolation
Feedback capacitor — optional bandwidth limiter to tame high-frequency harshness

dsp_model.description: High gain into hard clip function — diodes to ground produce abrupt, flat-topped clipping

behavioral_description: Hard clipping produces the most aggressive distortion character. Unlike soft clipping (diodes in feedback), here the op-amp output swings freely to full gain and the diodes to ground abruptly clamp the signal at the diode threshold. This creates a nearly square waveform at high gain settings, rich in odd-order harmonics that give a buzzy, aggressive edge. This is the topology of the ProCo RAT and MXR Distortion+. The feedback capacitor (Cf) is critical for taming the treble — without it, the harmonically rich hard-clipped signal can be painfully bright. At lower gain settings the transition region around the clipping threshold creates a crunchy, gritty texture. Hard clipping responds less to playing dynamics than soft clipping but delivers maximum sustain and saturation.
